title: Superflow Test Environment
description: Output of tools/detect-test-env.sh — read-only snapshot of the project's test infrastructure. Written atomically to .superflow/test-env.json. All fields are idempotent: re-running the script on the same environment produces byte-identical output. Additive-only evolution: new optional fields may be added without a version bump; removing or renaming fields is a breaking change.

docker.description: Docker daemon availability and runtime identity

present.description: True when the Docker CLI is found AND the daemon responds to `docker version` within 5 seconds

runtime.description: Detected Docker runtime. 'none' when Docker is absent or daemon is unreachable

ryuk_forced_disabled.description: True only for rootless Podman, which cannot run Ryuk's privileged container. Ryuk stays enabled everywhere else (including CI — see Testcontainers Ryuk policy). When true, callers must export TESTCONTAINERS_RYUK_DISABLED=true before running integration tests

exports.description: Environment variables required for Testcontainers to locate the Docker socket on non-Desktop runtimes. Empty object for Docker Desktop (uses the default socket path). On rootless Podman, also contains TESTCONTAINERS_RYUK_DISABLED

node.description: Node.js runtime and JavaScript test tooling

present.description: True when `node` is found on PATH

version.description: Raw output of `node --version` (e.g. 'v20.11.0'), or 'unknown' if the command timed out

runners.description: Test runners detected in package.json dependencies/devDependencies

playwright.description: Playwright JS package and browser availability

installed.description: True when node_modules/.bin/playwright exists (package is present in this project). Detection is read-only — no npx/auto-install is ever triggered

browsers.description: Browser names reported by `playwright install --list` as available. Empty when the package is absent or the binary is not executable

python.description: Python 3 runtime and Python test tooling

present.description: True when `python3` is found on PATH

version.description: Python version string without the 'Python ' prefix (e.g. '3.11.7'), or 'unknown' on timeout

runners.description: Python test runners detected in pyproject.toml or requirements*.txt

testcontainers.description: True when the testcontainers Python package is declared in pyproject.toml or any requirements*.txt

playwright.description: True when the playwright Python package is declared in requirements files OR `import playwright` succeeds at detection time

with_deps_supported.description: True when the host OS supports `playwright install --with-deps` (Debian/Ubuntu with sudo available). False on macOS and other distributions — use `playwright install <browser>` (binaries only) instead

project_type.description: 3-way project classifier used to determine which readiness layers are required. 'web': has a frontend framework (next/react/vue/svelte/astro/nuxt/gatsby/remix/vite/preact/solid-js/qwik/eleventy/vuepress/docusaurus/gridsome/@angular/*/@sveltejs/*/@remix-run/*/@builder.io/*/@11ty/*/@docusaurus/*) OR frontend directories (app/, pages/, src/routes/, src/pages/, src/app/) OR index.html OR SSG output dirs (dist/site/_site/out/build) containing *.html. Also 'web' when package.json has a scripts.start/dev/serve entry (ambiguous runnable JS app). 'backend-only': explicit server framework (express/fastify/koa/hapi/fastapi/flask/django) with no frontend signals. 'library': only when a POSITIVE library signal exists — pure npm package (main/module/exports/bin, no serve script), Python packaging project ([build-system]/setup.py/setup.cfg), or a repo with no package.json, no Python app/server signal, and no frontend/backend markers (e.g. a pure-Markdown tool). INVARIANT: ambiguous projects without a positive library signal always classify as 'web' to prevent silently skipping the E2E gate for real web apps

readiness.description: Machine-checkable verdict on testing layer availability, scoped to the detected project_type

unit.description: True when at least one UNIT test runner is detected: vitest or jest (Node) or pytest (Python). Playwright and Cypress are E2E tools and are explicitly excluded from this count — a project whose only runner is @playwright/test or cypress must still show unit=false

integration.description: True when unit=true AND docker.present=true (Testcontainers-style integration tests are possible)

e2e_tooling.description: True when the Playwright package is installed AND at least one browser is available locally. Does NOT verify the app starts — that is app_boot_smoke / P3 Release Gate territory

app_boot_smoke.description: Result of a best-effort app boot check. In Sprint A1 this is always 'skipped' — real boot verification is the P3 Release Gate's job

verdict.description: Overall readiness verdict scoped to project_type. 'ready': all required layers are available. 'partial': unit layer present but some layers missing. 'blocked': no unit runner found (cannot run any tests at all)

missing.description: Identifiers of missing layers / tools. Each item corresponds to an entry in recommendations

recommendations.description: Idempotent install commands that resolve each item in missing, in the same order. Run by the developer — never auto-executed by Superflow. On macOS (with_deps_supported=false), Playwright browser installs omit --with-deps
